message:
perf(types): skip TEXT UTF-8 revalidation on slot-equal fast path (OPT-6)

Profile showed core::str::converts::from_utf8 at 1.42% self-time on INSERT
hot path. The record-decode path re-validated every TEXT column's bytes as
UTF-8 via `std::str::from_utf8` on every read, even when the destination
`SqliteValue` slot already held an identical text value (a common case
with the thread-local value-pool slot reuse pattern — repeated enum-like
strings, or decoding the same row back into the same register).

Changes:

- Add `SmallText::as_bytes_direct()` which returns the raw UTF-8 bytes
  without going through `as_str()`. For the `Inline` variant, `as_str`
  itself runs `std::str::from_utf8` with an `.expect(...)`; by returning
  `&buf[..len]` directly we skip that validation when only byte-wise
  equality is needed. The returned slice is valid UTF-8 by construction,
  since every `SmallText` write path sources bytes from a `&str` or
  `Arc<str>`.

- Reorder the Text arm of `decode_value_into` in record.rs so the
  equal-bytes fast path runs BEFORE `from_utf8`. When the existing slot
  already holds a `SqliteValue::Text` whose bytes match the incoming
  record bytes, we can skip validation entirely — equal bytes to an
  already-validated value are equally valid. The validation is still
  performed on the non-matching-overwrite path and on the
  slot-not-yet-text path, so all correctness properties (rejecting
  malformed UTF-8 from untrusted on-disk records) are preserved.

- Add three regression tests: `decode_text_fast_path_equal_bytes_preserves_value`
  (verifies the fast path returns the correct string for both inline and
  heap SmallText variants), `decode_text_rejects_invalid_utf8_when_slot_differs`
  (verifies the non-fast-path still validates UTF-8), and
  `decode_text_rejects_invalid_utf8_when_slot_non_text` (verifies the
  slot-replacement path still validates UTF-8).

No unsafe (workspace forbids it). The invalid-UTF-8 rejection path in
`malformed_record_invalid_utf8_text` continues to pass, confirming that
untrusted bytes are still rejected — only the fast path for
slot-equal re-decodes is newly elided.

Test evidence:
- `cargo test -p fsqlite-types --lib`: 455 passed, 2 pre-existing
  failures unrelated to this change (`cx::tests::test_ambient_authority_audit_gate`
  flags pre-existing `Instant::now` usage in other crates;
  `opcode::tests::opcode_from_byte` unrelated).
- Workspace-wide `cargo check --workspace --tests` clean.
- `cargo clippy -p fsqlite-types --lib` clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
